sentence_reviews.text: Fairness and subgroup analysis measure performance, treatment, representation, and error disparities across protected or operationally meaningful groups.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF covers fairness, harmful bias, representativeness, validity, measurement, and impact across groups and contexts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Broadly supported by NIST fairness and bias governance concepts, but the exact list is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Coverage is necessarily incomplete: measured proxies, policies, and test suites cannot enumerate every deployment context or adversary. Its direct dependencies include Statistics & experimental design, Causal inference.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Fairness, bias & subgroup analysis is modeled as depending on Statistics & experimental design, Causal inference and feeding downstream nodes such as Medical imaging & clinical decision support, Tabular predictive analytics & decision support.
